usage_modes: standalone: invoked directly with a PR number and known gap, outside the release notes workflow
workflow: runs as Phase 1.75 of the release notes workflow, consuming the handoff table produced by docs-pr-check (Phase 1.5)

evals.prompt: PR #5962 added a new Accept: application/llm header to the Tempo HTTP API but the API docs don't cover it. Use docs-pr-write to update the docs.
evals.expected_output: The API docs page is updated to document the new Accept header value and its behavior. Content is added to the existing page without restructuring it. Includes a concrete HTTP request or curl example showing the header, explains what response format is returned. Return format shows 'Files changed', what was documented and where, and any open items.
assertions: A file under docs/sources/tempo/ appears in 'Files changed'
New content includes an HTTP request or curl example showing the Accept header
Existing page structure and other API entries are preserved
Output includes a section mapping PR #5962 to the file that was updated
Output includes an 'Open items' section (even if empty)

evals.prompt: Run docs-pr-write for PR #6103. There are no existing docs for the integer dedicated columns feature and I need a new configuration section added.
evals.expected_output: The relevant configuration docs page is updated with a new section covering integer dedicated columns. Content explains what the feature does in user terms, includes a YAML configuration example with field names verified against the codebase, and notes the Tempo version where it was introduced. Return format shows 'Files changed', PR-to-doc mapping, and open items.
assertions: Output includes a 'Files changed' section listing at least one path under docs/sources/tempo/
Output includes a mapping of PR #6103 to the file(s) updated
Documented content includes a YAML configuration example
Content notes the minimum Tempo version or states 'introduced in Tempo X.Y'
Output includes an 'Open items' section (even if empty)
Configuration field names in the example match code, not just the PR description

evals.prompt:
We're in Phase 1.75 of the Tempo 3.0 release notes workflow. docs-pr-check (Phase 1.5) produced the following handoff. Run docs-pr-write to implement the required docs. Target branch is main.

#6103 | Docs needed | No existing docs found for integer dedicated columns feature; new configuration section required | docs/sources/tempo/configuration/_index.md
evals.expected_output: The configuration docs page is updated with a new section covering integer dedicated columns. Content explains the feature in user terms, includes a verified YAML example, and notes the Tempo version. Return format includes 'Files changed', PR-to-doc mapping for #6103, and an 'Open items' section. Output is structured so the release notes writer can link to the new docs section in Phase 2.
assertions: Output includes a 'Files changed' section listing at least one path under docs/sources/tempo/
Output includes a PR-to-doc mapping with an entry for #6103
Documented content includes a YAML configuration example
Content notes the minimum Tempo version or states 'introduced in Tempo X.Y'
Output includes an 'Open items' section (even if empty)
Configuration field names in the example are verified against code, not copied verbatim from the PR description

evals.prompt:
Phase 1.5 of the Tempo 3.0 release notes workflow is complete. Here is the docs-pr-check handoff for Phase 1.75. Run docs-pr-write. Branch context is release-3.0.

#5962 | Docs update needed | API docs missing documentation for new Accept: application/llm header behavior | docs/sources/tempo/api_docs/_index.md
evals.expected_output: The API docs page is updated to document the new Accept header value. Content is inserted into the existing page without restructuring it. Includes a concrete HTTP request example, explains the response format, and cross-links to related pages if they exist. Return format shows 'Files changed', PR-to-doc mapping for #5962, and 'Open items'. Existing API entries and page structure are preserved.
assertions: The file docs/sources/tempo/api_docs/_index.md (or equivalent API reference file) appears in 'Files changed'
Output includes a PR-to-doc mapping entry for #5962
New content includes an HTTP request or curl example showing the Accept header
Existing page structure and other API entries are preserved (agent did not rewrite the whole page)
Output includes an 'Open items' section
